audit_log.detail: missing_information:Capture Baseline Knee ROM and Pain
missing_information:Document Functional Limitation Related to Knee

subjective.chief_complaint: Patient reports knee pain requiring therapeutic exercise intervention.

objective.observation_notes: Therapeutic exercise performed for right knee. Specific exercise details, sets/reps, resistance level, and patient tolerance not documented in session transcript.
objective.range_of_motion: Baseline knee range of motion (flexion/extension) not documented in session

assessment.diagnosis_summary: Patient presented with right knee pain. Therapeutic exercise was initiated as skilled intervention. However, critical baseline measurements including pain scale (0-10), active/passive knee ROM (flexion/extension in degrees), and manual muscle testing grades were not documented prior to exercise. Functional limitations related to ADLs (walking, stairs, transfers) were not captured. These baseline measures are necessary to establish medical necessity, justify skilled therapy, and measure progress toward functional goals. Patient response to today's exercise intervention and any symptom changes are not documented. Documentation gaps to address: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise..

plan.follow_up_plan: Continue therapeutic exercise for right knee. Recommend: (1) Establish and document baseline pain scale (0-10), active and passive knee flexion/extension ROM in degrees, and manual muscle testing grades at next visit; (2) Document specific exercises prescribed, number of sets/reps, resistance level, and patient response; (3) Capture functional limitations and ADL impact to support medical necessity; (4) Home exercise program details and patient education not documented — recommend clarification at next visit; (5) Follow-up frequency and duration not specified — recommend clinician review and documentation. Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise.

compliance_gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.
Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise.

patient_summary.summary:
Hi Jane! Today we worked on therapeutic exercises to help with your right knee pain. To make sure we're tracking your progress and giving you the best care, we need to document some important baseline measurements at your next visit — like your current pain level, how far you can bend and straighten your knee, and which daily activities are most affected by the knee pain. Please continue with any exercises we discussed, and let us know if your symptoms improve or change. We're here to help you get back to your normal activities!

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

assistant_suggestions.title: Capture Baseline Knee ROM and Pain
assistant_suggestions.body:
Document active and/or passive range of motion (flexion/extension) in degrees and current pain level (0-10 scale) for the right knee before exercise to establish baseline and justify medical necessity.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

assistant_suggestions.title: Record Specific Exercise Details
assistant_suggestions.body:
Note the exact exercises performed, number of sets/reps, resistance level (if any), and patient response (improved/maintained/worsened symptoms) during the session.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

assistant_suggestions.title: Document Functional Limitation Related to Knee
assistant_suggestions.body:
Capture how the right knee pain affects the patient's daily activities (walking, stairs, ADLs) to support medical necessity and functional goals.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
assistant_suggestions.disclaimer: AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
